description: createBookmark() silently fabricates a fake local bookmark and returns ok:true on any non-400 failure (network error or DB-down 503), causing BookmarkNewView to redirect to /bookmarks as if the bookmark was saved when nothing was actually persisted, violating the spec's requirement that a graceful error state be shown when the database is unreachable during create.

The nginx SPA fallback (try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html) serves HTTP 200 with index.html for any unmatched path such as /foo instead of a real 404, and no backend explicit-route/404 handling exists anywhere, violating the spec's 'Unknown route returns 404' scenario and the plan's explicit no-catch-all requirement.

Production build (colossus.stack.json build command and web/Dockerfile.frontend both run bare `npx vite build`) never passes --base=/e2e-lane-vue-nest/, and vite.config.ts does not hardcode base, so shipped assets are built with base '/' and will 404 through the ingress which strips the /e2e-lane-vue-nest/ prefix before forwarding, breaking asset loading and deep-linking.

listBookmarks() never rethrows on network/DB failure — it always resolves with fabricated seed data — making BookmarksView's 'error' state and bookmarks-error testid unreachable dead code, so backend-down is masked as fake data behind an offline banner rather than a true error state.

Admin login (and LoginView/SignupView similarly) treats any non-401/409 failure, including backend-down/network errors, as a successful login by storing a fabricated 'preview-admin-token' and navigating to /admin/settings, masking real outages as successful authentication.

The root Dockerfile still builds the original nginx 'Placeholder deployment' static page and was never replaced with a build that ships the real backend+frontend, and k8s/deployment.yaml still defines only a single container from that placeholder image with no wiring for the actual two-service (backend:3000 + web nginx-proxy:80) topology that was built — current deploy artifacts would fail .colossus-acceptance.json's own reject_signatures check for 'Placeholder deployment' (may be regenerated by a separate deploy-agent step outside coder scope; flagged for confirmation).

Bookmark rows render only the URL's hostname (e.g. 'vuejs.org') via hostname(b.url), not the full URL text, which may not satisfy the spec's 'each bookmark as a row showing its title and URL' if verification asserts the literal URL string is visible.
